perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding anything in this Agreement to the contrary, it is acknowledged and agreed that there is no minimum amount of Shipper's Product that must be delivered to the System hereunder; accordingly, Shipper shall not have any liability (financial or otherwise) for failure to deliver any particular quantity of Product to Carrier; provided, however, the foregoing sentence shall not reduce or eliminate Shipper's obligation to pay the fees provided herein to the extent Services are provided hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding anything in this Agreement to the contrary, it is acknowledged and agreed that Shipper shall deliver a minimum amount of 10,000 Barrels of Shipper's Product to the System hereunder per month; accordingly, Shipper shall have liability (financial or otherwise) for failure to deliver particular quantity of Product to Carrier; provided, however, the foregoing sentence shall not reduce or eliminate Shipper's obligation to pay the fees provided herein to the extent Services are provided hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces a contradiction by stating that the Shipper shall deliver a minimum amount of 10,000 Barrels of Shipper's Product. This is in direct conflict with the prior statement in the original section. The original text says there is no minimum amount of product that must be delivered. Now there is a fixed minimum, creating uncertainty about the Shipper's obligations. It is contradicting within the Section 7.1 where it states the initial definition and in the Tariff where the rates are defined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Dedication. During the Term, subject to the terms of the AMI/MEA Agreement, Shipper dedicates and commits to this Agreement, and shall deliver or cause to be delivered to the System for transportation hereunder, all Shipper Product (the "Dedication"); provided, however, if Carrier is unable to provide Services with respect to all or any volume of Shipper Product, then such volume will be temporarily released from the Dedication until Carrier provides Shipper written notice that Carrier will be able to resume receiving such volume hereunder. Notwithstanding anything in this Agreement to the contrary, it is acknowledged and agreed that Shipper shall deliver a minimum amount of 10,000 Barrels of Shipper's Product to the System hereunder per month; accordingly, Shipper shall have liability (financial or otherwise) for failure to deliver particular quantity of Product to Carrier; provided, however, the foregoing sentence shall not reduce or eliminate Shipper's obligation to pay the fees provided herein to the extent Services are provided hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original agreement by introducing a minimum delivery requirement of 10,000 barrels per month. The original text explicitly states that there is no minimum amount. This new minimum delivery requirement directly conflicts with the prior 'no minimum' clause, creating a contradiction in Section 7.1 and impacting the Tariff where rates are defined based on assumed volumes. The contradicted text itself contains the conflicting statement, making the contradiction very clear.

perturbation.original_text: Carrier reserves the right to modify or amend the Tariff, in its sole discretion, as it deems necessary; provided, however, any such modification or amendment shall be consistent with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Carrier reserves the right to modify or amend the Tariff, in its sole discretion, as it deems necessary; however, any such modification or amendment shall contradict with this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction. The original text states that Tariff modifications must be consistent with the Agreement. Now it states that modifications shall contradict. This introduces confusion about the relationship between the Agreement and the Tariff. This change directly affects the interpretation of Section 15.1 of the contract and potentially all other sections which rely on the Tariff.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Common Carrier Pipeline. The System will be operated as a common carrier pipeline, and Shipper's rights hereunder shall be subject to all laws related to and governing the operation of common carrier pipelines, including, without limitation, laws and regulations that prevent discrimination in favor of any given shipper or the provision of service for consideration other than the rate set forth in a published tariff. The Tariff shall apply to the Services provided hereunder, and if there is a conflict between a provision of this Agreement and the Tariff, the Tariff shall apply. Carrier reserves the right to modify or amend the Tariff, in its sole discretion, as it deems necessary; however, any such modification or amendment shall contradict with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself. The original text stated that Tariff modifications must be consistent with the Agreement. The changed text explicitly states that modifications 'shall contradict with this Agreement.' This introduces a direct in-text contradiction within the single section itself, making it internally inconsistent and contradicting itself, and creating confusion about the relationship between the Agreement and the Tariff. The Contradicted Text also contains the changed text, reinforcing that a contradiction exists within this text, and thus within the document.

perturbation.original_text: Financial Assurances. If Shipper has failed to pay any amount when due under this Agreement and if such non-payment is not being disputed in good faith by Shipper, Carrier shall have the right to request and receive from Shipper adequate assurance of performance ("Financial Assurance") which shall mean credit support in a form reasonably acceptable to Carrier and in an amount and for the term reasonably acceptable to Carrier.
perturbation.changed_text: Financial Assurances. If Shipper has failed to pay any amount when due under this Agreement and if such non-payment is not being disputed in good faith by Shipper, Carrier shall have the right to request and receive from Shipper adequate assurance of performance ("Financial Assurance") which shall mean credit support in a form not acceptable to Carrier and in an amount and for the term not acceptable to Carrier.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation directly contradicts the original meaning of the Financial Assurances section. It changes 'reasonably acceptable' to 'not acceptable', making it unclear what financial assurances, if any, the Shipper can provide that would satisfy the Carrier. This contradiction specifically affects the enforceability of financial assurance obligations and creates ambiguity in Section 13.1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Financial Assurances. If Shipper has failed to pay any amount when due under this Agreement and if such non-payment is not being disputed in good faith by Shipper, Carrier shall have the right to request and receive from Shipper adequate assurance of performance ("Financial Assurance") which shall mean credit support in a form not acceptable to Carrier and in an amount and for the term not acceptable to Carrier. Any of the following shall be an acceptable form of credit support: (a) An irrevocable standby letter of credit from a bank satisfactory to Carrier; 14 Source: RANGE RESOURCES - LOUISIANA, INC., 8-K, 4/17/2015 (b) Provide a prepayment or a deposit in advance of the Month in which Services hereunder are to be provided; or (c) A performance bond issued by a Person satisfactory to Carrier. If the credit of Shipper's guarantor is satisfactory in Carrier's opinion, a demand for Financial Assurance can be satisfied with a guarantee issued on behalf of Shipper in a form acceptable to Carrier, but only for as long as the credit of Shipper's guarantor continues to be acceptable to Carrier. Carrier acknowledges and agrees that Memorial Resource Development Corp. is a satisfactory guarantor. Should Shipper or its guarantor fail to provide Financial Assurance within ten (10) Business Days after receipt of written demand for such assurance, then Carrier shall have the right to suspend performance under this Agreement until such time as Shipper furnishes Financial Assurance. For the avoidance of doubt, such suspension of performance by Carrier shall not relieve Shipper of its obligation to make payments of amounts due hereunder, including, without limitation, payment of fees due hereunder. If during the Term, Carrier has failed to pay any amount when due under this Agreement and if such non-payment is not being disputed in good faith by Carrier, Shipper shall have the right to request and receive from Carrier adequate Financial Assurance under similar terms and conditions as described above, including the right to suspend performance under this Agreement until such time as Carrier furnishes Financial Assurance.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the definition of 'Financial Assurance' from 'reasonably acceptable' to 'not acceptable' to the Carrier. This directly contradicts the subsequent clauses in the contradicted text that specify 'acceptable form of credit support' and outline examples such as 'a bank satisfactory to Carrier', 'credit of Shipper's guarantor is satisfactory in Carrier's opinion' and 'in a form acceptable to Carrier'. The change creates an irreconcilable conflict, making it impossible to determine what would constitute a valid Financial Assurance, thereby undermining the enforceability of the financial assurance provisions.
